payload.subject: Work order: full Sonnet re-score (998 texts × 9 dimensions)
payload.summary: The scorer comparison (turn 11) confirmed Sonnet produces better labels than Haiku across 8/10 dimensions. CC and DA have been dropped (construct problems). This message requests the psq-agent to execute the full Sonnet re-score — the critical-path item that unblocks DistilBERT retraining and factor analysis.

work_order.task: Score all 998 texts across 9 viable dimensions using claude-sonnet-4-6

excluded_dimensions.contractual_clarity: Construct problem — 51% pile-up even with Sonnet, inter-scorer r=0.644

implementation_guidance.existing_script: safety-quotient/data/separated_scoring/score_subset_sonnet.sh scores 100 texts. Adapt to score all 998 texts per dimension.
adaptation_steps: 2. Change SUBSET_FILE reference to use full batch file texts (not scorer_comparison_subset.json)
3. Remove the 100-text subset filter — score all texts in each {dim}.json batch file
4. Update output filename pattern: {dim}_sonnet_full_scores.json (distinguish from subset scores)
5. Keep the same v2 anti-midpoint prompt (already in score_subset_sonnet.sh)
7. Add resumability: check existing output file count before starting each dim
8. Remove CC (contractual_clarity) and DA (defensive_architecture) from ALL_DIMS array
implementation_guidance.prompt_template: Use the identical v2 anti-midpoint prompt from score_subset_sonnet.sh. Do NOT modify the scoring guidance — consistency with the 100-text comparison is essential for validating that full-corpus results match subset findings.
implementation_guidance.output_format: Same as subset: {text_id: {score: N, confidence: N}} per dimension JSON file
implementation_guidance.rate_limiting: 3s between batches, 5s between dimensions. ~450 calls total at batch size 20. Estimated wall time: 2-3 hours with delays.

after_completion: 1. Verify each dimension has 998 scores (no gaps)
2. Compare pile-up rates against the 100-text subset results — should be within ±5 percentage points
3. Check that no dimension has pile-up > 40% (would indicate prompt or parsing failure)
4. Report per-dimension: count, mean, std, pile-up %, min, max

downstream_tasks.task: Retrain DistilBERT v29+ on Sonnet labels

claims.text: score_subset_sonnet.sh successfully scored 100 texts × 11 dimensions with Sonnet during Session 34.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation — script ran to completion, results analyzed in scorer_comparison.py.

claims.text: Estimated cost of $15-20 for 998 × 9 dims at ~500 tokens per scoring call.
claims.confidence_basis: Extrapolation from 100-text subset cost. Actual cost depends on text length variance and Sonnet pricing.

action_gate.gate_condition: User terminal access required to run the scoring script

epistemic_flags: Cost estimate is approximate — depends on text length distribution and current Sonnet API pricing
Wall time estimate assumes no rate limit errors or retries
